principle: Do not equate valid JSON, recognized containers, source-lane presence, or UI availability with trusted biblical text.

requires: Keep active boundary and schema/container guards passing
notes: This does not assert a fresh scholarly full collation of every NT verse.
It asserts the currently named canonical NT corpus defects are repaired or overlay-represented without main-row insertion.

reasons: NABRE OT alignment/versification defects remain unresolved.
Daniel and related Greek-extension handling require source/versification decisions.
Minor-prophet and chapter-boundary issues require bounded inspection before patching.
canonical_ot.nextDecision: Design OT versification/remap strategy using translation overlays rather than ad hoc row insertion.

reasons: Psalms use a legacy root-array subsystem.
Psalms require separate architecture before normalization or trust claims.
Psalms include non-standard extended range material that must be governed deliberately.
psalms.nextDecision: Treat Psalms as its own corpus architecture track.

reasons: Several files are structural stubs or materially incomplete.
3 and 4 Maccabees are known severe completeness risks.
Some deuterocanonical files require rebuild, quarantine, or explicit scope declaration.
deuterocanon.nextDecision: Decide rebuild versus quarantine lane before text repair.

reasons: Odes and related canticle files are structurally identified but underpopulated.
Some metadata overstates completeness or liturgical status.
broader_canon.nextDecision: Classify each broader-canon file as trusted, scaffold, excerpt, devotional/liturgical, or quarantine.

reasons: Vulgate work is protected active work and intentionally excluded from this remediation thread.
